title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications - Microsoft Research
gist: Microsoft released Aurora 1.5, an open-source Earth-system foundation model that adds 22 weather variables, hourly resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting, and claims it beats the state-of-the-art ECMWF ensemble on 88.9% of evaluated targets while cutting tropical-cyclone track error by roughly a third versus the original Aurora.

insights.claim: Aurora 1.5 expands an Earth-system foundation model from 4 to 26 weather variables (22 new), adds hourly temporal resolution, and introduces probabilistic ensemble forecasting.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: A single foundation model now covers energy, agriculture, transport, and climate-risk signals, expanding the addressable enterprise market for AI weather intelligence.
insights.quote: The breadth update adds 22 new variables to Aurora's original 4, including representative surface, pressure-level, wind, temperature, humidity, precipitation, and radiation fields.
insights.locator: The breadth update adds 22 new variables

insights.claim: Hourly temporal resolution is added to enable precision operational guidance such as timing the onset of precipitation, trade decisions, or a landfalling tropical cyclone.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Finer time granularity is what makes an AI weather model usable for time-sensitive commercial decisions like power trading, not just research.
insights.locator: The update to hourly temporal resolution

insights.claim: Ensemble forecasting was one of the most requested user capabilities, because forecasts are sensitive to initial conditions and model uncertainty and ensembles show the range and likelihood of outcomes rather than a single estimate.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: For power, transport, and risk applications the forecast distribution matters as much as the point estimate, signaling where value accrues in weather AI.
insights.quote: For a multitude of applications including power systems, transport, agriculture, extreme-weather planning, and climate risk, the model distribution matters as much as the best estimate.
insights.locator: The ensemble version of Aurora 1.5 introduces stochastic perturbations

insights.claim: Aurora 1.5's probabilistic forecasts outperform the state-of-the-art ECMWF dynamical ensemble (ENS) on 88.9% of evaluated variable-and-lead-time targets across upper-air geopotential, temperature, humidity, and five surface variables.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: A credible claim of beating ECMWF, the incumbent physics benchmark, on nearly 90% of targets is the key proof point that AI ensembles can rival operational dynamical models.
insights.quote: Aurora 1.5 outperforms ECMWF ENS on 88.9% of the evaluated variable-and-lead-time targets.

insights.claim: Evaluated on all 2024-2025 tropical cyclones, Aurora 1.5's ensemble median cut track error by roughly one-third versus the original Aurora, reaching that gain by day 5.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Substantial storm-track improvement is directly monetizable for insurers, energy operators, and emergency planners exposed to hurricane risk.
insights.quote: roughly one-third lower track error when comparing the ensemble median to the original Aurora

insights.claim: For Hurricane Helene, Aurora 1.5's probabilistic ensemble forecast (from 0 UTC September 24, 2024) enveloped the verified storm track, capturing uncertainty in the storm's progression.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: A concrete high-impact-event example is the kind of evidence enterprise buyers need before trusting AI forecasts operationally.

insights.claim: Aurora 1.5's ensemble was built via multi-stage fine-tuning on the original Aurora: expanding variables and adding hourly resolution, injecting controlled perturbations into the latent conditioning pathway, then auto-regressive fine-tuning on ECMWF HRES analysis data from 2018 to 2023.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: The recipe shows probabilistic skill was engineered on top of a pretrained base rather than trained from scratch, a template competitors can copy or challenge.
insights.quote: the team introduced controlled perturbations into the model's latent conditioning pathway and optimized the ensemble for probabilistic forecast quality.
insights.locator: This ensemble capability was developed through multi-stage fine-tuning

insights.claim: Aurora is a single foundation model first published in Nature in 2025 that can be adapted to medium-range weather, ocean waves, atmospheric chemistry, and high-resolution forecasting through fine-tuning.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: A general-purpose Earth-system foundation model that fine-tunes to many domains mirrors the LLM playbook and hints at platform economics for weather AI.

insights.claim: Aurora 1.5 is released open source on GitHub with checkpoints on Hugging Face, while Microsoft Weather monetizes it via managed services, data, cloud infrastructure, and operational support for enterprises.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: This is an open-core commercial model: give away the weights to seed adoption, capture revenue on the operational and infrastructure layer.
insights.quote: Microsoft Weather's role is to connect that open research foundation with the data, infrastructure, and applied workflows required by enterprises to use weather intelligence responsibly and with confidence.
insights.locator: Released as open source on GitHub

insights.claim: Aurora's platform path routes the model into Microsoft Foundry and Planetary Computer Pro, alongside Agent skills and Azure services that connect it with geospatial data and scalable infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Weather AI is being wired into Microsoft's broader Azure and Foundry stack, another workload driving cloud consumption.
insights.locator: Connecting open models to operational use

insights.claim: Microsoft Weather plans to extend Aurora in the coming months with additional fit-for-purpose AI weather models designed for enterprise scenarios prioritizing forecast quality, speed, uncertainty, and decision support.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: A pipeline of specialized enterprise weather models signals sustained commercial investment, not a one-off research release.

insights.claim: Swiss utility BKW is using Aurora 1.5 alongside existing Microsoft Weather models to support energy operations where weather-dependent generation and infrastructure planning must come together.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Renewable-heavy utilities are early paying customers for probabilistic weather AI to manage generation variability.
insights.quote: In a time of accelerated transformation, it supports our ambition to operate increasingly renewable-based systems, where generation is inherently weather-dependent
insights.locator: BKW provides an early proof point

insights.claim: Terradot, a Microsoft Climate Innovation Fund portfolio company, is using Aurora-derived weather representations in TerraNova to estimate and optimize carbon dioxide removal from enhanced rock weathering under field conditions.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Aurora's outputs feed carbon-removal verification, expanding the model's use beyond forecasting into the carbon-credit and climate-tech value chain.
insights.quote: By building on Aurora, we're significantly advancing our R&D timelines and accelerating our path towards gigaton-scale carbon removal.
insights.locator: Beyond weather: Aurora as an Earth-system foundation

insights.claim: The UK Met Office is exploring Aurora to see how foundation models can work alongside physics-based systems and translate AI weather prediction into climate projection.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: National meteorological agency validation lends credibility and points toward a future of AI-based climate models, not just weather.
insights.quote: Aurora is a great platform for learning how to translate these tools for use in climate projection to make the AI climate models of the future.
insights.locator: Aurora is also being explored with partners

insights.claim: Microsoft Weather powers weather across Windows, Bing, Copilot, Edge, and MSN, reaching more than a billion devices across 180 countries, and was ranked the world's most accurate global forecast provider by an independent third party for three consecutive years, 2022-2024.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: Established distribution and a top accuracy ranking give Microsoft a strong incumbency position to commercialize enterprise weather AI.
insights.quote: ranked the world's most accurate global forecast provider by an independent third party for three consecutive years from 2022 to 2024

insights.claim: Microsoft argues foundation models should complement rather than replace physics-based models and domain expertise, emphasizing careful evaluation and transparency.
insights.speaker: Brenda Potts
insights.so_what: The hybrid positioning tempers hype and targets integration alongside trusted numerical weather prediction rather than a rip-and-replace pitch.
insights.quote: Foundation models should complement—not replace—physics-based models and domain expertise.
insights.locator: From open research to broader impact
